'''Matty Gee''' (born 12 December 1994) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He has played for the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]], and the [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] and  [[Workington Town]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Gee has played for the [[London Broncos]] in the Championship and the Super League, and on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[London Skolars]] in League 1. He has also played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League|top flight]].

==Background==
Gee was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester, England]].<ref name="Matty Gee on rugby league journey, Tony Smith the salesman and his ambitions for Hull KR">{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-matty-gee-ambitions-3418953|title=Matty Gee on rugby league journey, Tony Smith the salesman and his ambitions for Hull KR|publisher=Hull Daily Mail|date=2019-10-12|accessdate=2021-01-04}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Salford Red Devils===
Gee played for the [[Salford Red Devils|Red Devils]] in the Super League.

In 2014 he was loaned to [[North Wales Crusaders|North Wales]] in League 1.

In 2015 he was loaned to [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] in the Championship.

In 2016 he was loaned to the [[North Wales Crusaders|Crusaders]] in League 1. Later in 2016 he was loaned to [[Workington Town|Workington]] in the Championship.{{cn|date=March 2024}}

===London Broncos===
Gee played for the [[London Broncos]] in the Championship and the top flight, and spent time on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[London Skolars]] in [[2017 League 1|League 1]].<ref name="Workington Town sign Salford's Matt Gee on loan">{{cite web|url= http://www.newsandstar.co.uk/sport/Workington-Town-sign-Salfords-Matt-Gee-on-loan-85605c7b-78b2-4628-92a7-d6c3228eaa37-ds|title=Workington Town sign Salford's Matt Gee on loan|publisher=News & Star|date=2016-04-07|accessdate=2016-09-12}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
After London's final-day points difference relegation from the Super League, Gee left the capital and moved to Hull KR ahead of the [[2020 Super League season]].{{cn|date=March 2024}}

===Leigh Centurions===
On 6 October 2020 it was announced that Gee would join the [[Leigh Centurions]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-forward-matty-gee-joins-leigh/|title=Hull KR forward Matty Gee joins Leigh|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=6 Oct 2020|accessdate=23 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Halifax Panthers===
On 1 November 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 Nov 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title=Halifax confirm signing of Leigh forward|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/halifax-sign-leigh-forward-matty-gee/}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 9 Aug 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Bradford Bulls|date=9 Aug 2024|accessdate=31 Oct 2024|title=BULLS SIGN GEE ON TWO YEAR DEAL|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/2669/bulls-sign-gee-on-two-year-deal}}</ref>

In October 2025 it was reported that he had retired.
